signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hawkishness
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.4713 — NZD outperformance (1W avg +1.152%) vs CHF weakness (1W avg -0.657%) creates a 1.81% G10 spread. Despite a -0.58% real rate, NZD is supported by RBNZ hawkishness and a +250bp OIS carry advantage over SNB. Retail sentiment is at an 87% short extreme on AUD/CHF, suggesting broader CHF-cross short-covering risk if the NZD/CHF bilateral theme persists. RBNZ 2.5%.
signals.watch_level: RBNZ meeting on 2 Sep for hike confirmation

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Volatility + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.3 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite the pair testing levels well above the 160.00 intervention threshold. Options market shows a moderate put skew (25d RR at -1.42) and elevated vol premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.5%), signaling institutional hedging against a sharp JPY reversal. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 remains crowded, while USD CESI (+57) supports the greenback against JPY's safe-haven function, which remains impaired (z=-1.13σ vs VIX).
signals.watch_level: BoC Monetary Policy Report at 10:45 for broader USD sentiment shift

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + BoC MPR Risk
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4050 — The pair exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (corr -0.918 vs norm -0.001, z=-2.00σ), with CAD failing to track the +1.34% intraday rise in crude. COT LF net short -87,846 (22.6% long) reflects heavy spec positioning against the CAD ahead of today's BoC Monetary Policy Report. A +150bp USD carry advantage persists, but the extreme correlation deviation suggests CAD is pricing in a dovish BoC shift rather than energy fundamentals.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Volatility Premium
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1429 — EUR 1W avg -0.346% reflects persistent data misses (CESI -13) and ECB Nagel's signal that rates are at an 'appropriate level.' Options market prices an elevated premium (VRP +2.1%) with a mild put skew (25d RR -0.57), while COT LF net short -66,057 shows specs are already positioned for downside. The +150bp USD carry advantage remains the structural ceiling for EUR recovery.
